normalized_title: Development Control Policy 2.6 Residential Road Planning
scope_summary: Sets out the WAPC's requirements and design guidelines for the planning, design and layout of roads within residential neighbourhoods in Western Australia, covering the two lower tiers of the DC 1.4 road hierarchy (local distributors and access roads, including access ways, access places, access lanes and rear laneways). It addresses traffic volume and speed criteria, reserve and carriageway widths, neighbourhood walkability benchmarks, pedestrian/cyclist systems, public transport provision and transport management studies for residential subdivision design. Adopted in 1988 and updated/reconsidered by the Commission in June 1998.

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum design traffic volume for local distributors
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: local distributor roads (no direct lot access above this)

design operating speed for access ways
maximum traffic volume on access ways
maximum distance from most distant lot on an access way to the local distributor
maximum traffic volume on access places
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: access places (designed for 20 km/h operating speeds)

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum traffic volume on access lanes and rear laneways
vehicle operating speed on access lanes and rear laneways
maximum reserve width for access lanes (reducible to 6 m)
maximum dwelling distance to public open space
maximum dwelling distance to primary school
maximum walking distance to a bus stop

quality_flags: vertical sidebar banner text extracted as one-character-per-line strings at page starts ('RESIDENTIAL ROAD PLANNING POLICY NO. DC 2.6')
two-column PDF text interleaved in places - some numeric standards (reserve width bullets) are hard to attribute to an exact clause
figures/diagrams (neighbourhood cell types, frontage management techniques) extracted as fragmentary text including (cid:N) glyph artifacts
policy dates from June 1998; Liveable Neighbourhoods and later operational policies have partially overtaken it in practice - verify currency before relying on standards
